channel.title: Unexplained Phenomena on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Unexplained Phenomena on Alien Truth Social

item.title: The Unexplained Files
item.description:
The Unexplained Files is an American documentary television series that aired on Science Channel. The series investigated paranormal and unexplained phenomena across two seasons, with the first season having 8 episodes and the second season planning 12. While it was the most watched series on the Science Channel during its first season, critics noted its tendency to focus on sensationalized theories over scientific explanations. One review highlighted how a veterinarian’s explanation for cattle mutilations was given minimal airtime compared to “outlandish theories,” leading to criticism of the show as biased.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Unexplained_Files

item.title: Betz mystery sphere
item.description:
The Betz mystery sphere, an approximately 8-inch metal sphere, was discovered by the Betz family in Florida in 1974. The sphere reportedly reacted to sound, rolled on its own, and changed direction, leading Terry Betz to conduct experiments with it. At the time of discovery, the United States Navy suggested it was a ball bearing from a chemical plant’s piping system, and the Miami Herald reported a similar object in Jacksonville was identified as part of a paper mill valve. Later analysis by Skeptoid suggested the sphere was a ball check valve produced by Bell & Howell and proposed that uneven floors could explain its apparent autonomous movement.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betz_mystery_sphere

item.title: Gabriel L. 'Gabe' Valdez: Investigator of Cattle Mutilations
item.description:
Gabriel L. “Gabe” Valdez was a New Mexico State Police officer and Gaming Control Board investigator known for leading the criminal investigation into livestock mutilations in 1970s New Mexico. During his investigations, Valdez reported a mysterious aircraft and the discovery of tranquilizing and blood-removing drugs in mutilated animal remains. His son later stated that Valdez believed the mutilations were a government testing program, not alien activity. Valdez passed away on August 7, 2011.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gabe_Valdez

item.title: Dancing Blue Lights at Silver Cliff Cemetery
item.description:
Silver Cliff Cemetery, established in the 1880s near Silver Cliff, Colorado, is divided into Catholic and Protestant sections. The cemetery is known for reports of “dancing blue lights” that have been observed since the 1880s. These lights, described as blue lantern lights or white spheres, were featured in National Geographic Magazine in August 1969. Researcher Karen Stollznow suggested natural explanations for the lights, such as visual phenomena related to the human eye.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silver_Cliff_Cemetery
